headlines.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
headlines.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

headlines.title: Sarah Ferguson is lying low, but pressure is mounting for her to give evidence on Epstein
headlines.description: There are calls for the former duchess to give testimony in the US, although there is no legal requirement she does so.

headlines.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors, jury finds
headlines.description: A San Francisco jury concludes Musk’s claims in 2022 were damaging to a group of Twitter investors.

headlines.title: Chuck Norris memes were undefeated for a generation of young fans
headlines.description: Chuck Norris became an inadvertent source of humour for a viral online celebration of his movie tough guy persona.

headlines.title: Traitors and Idris Elba help Comic Relief raise £30m
headlines.description: The annual charity telethon was hosted by Davina McCall and featured appearances from Idris Elba, Katherine Ryan and Catherine Tate.

headlines.title: Five questions that still need answering about the meningitis outbreak
headlines.description: The size and speed of the outbreak which has now affected 29 people, killing two of them, has been labelled "unprecedented".

headlines.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
headlines.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

headlines.title: Watch: Israeli strike next to British journalist is not AI-generated
headlines.description: The video of the strike, hitting just metres from RT's Steve Sweeney and his crew, was recorded on Thursday in southern Lebanon.

headlines.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as gas prices soar
headlines.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

headlines.title: Saturday Night Live UK is here - but can it make you laugh?
headlines.description: The first episode of the British version of the American sketch show hits screens this weekend, and might face a tough crowd.

headlines.title: Paying back my student loan is more painful now I have a young family
headlines.description: Graduates, parents and students spoke to BBC Your Voice about their experiences of student loans.

headlines.title: Home working, long leases and rise of parking apps - what went wrong for NCP
headlines.description: How could a company that charged as much as £65 for a day's parking fail to turn a profit?

headlines.title: Russia's school propaganda was  highlighted by Oscar-winning film - but does it work?
headlines.description: The messages are clear: the full-scale invasion is a defensive war and patriotism means unquestionable loyalty.

headlines.title: The fight to control the narrative in the Afghan-Pakistan conflict
headlines.description: The battle between the neighbouring countries is taking place as much online as it is on the ground.

headlines.title: A drug transformed my life. Now it's derailed my Australian dream
headlines.description: Regan Sparks, who has cystic fibrosis, takes medication that means she is not eligible for a visa.

headlines.title: 'UK facing new tax hikes' and 'My Ricky's last hug'
headlines.description: Iran war could lead to UK tax rises and boxer Ricky Hutton's death inquest leads Saturdays' papers.

headlines.title: BTS comeback concert: Seoul gets ready to host 260,000 fans
headlines.description: RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook will perform together for the first time since October 2022.

headlines.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer actor Nicholas Brendon dies aged 54
headlines.description: Brendon died of "natural cases", his family said in a statement, remembering him as "passionate" and "sensitive".

headlines.title: Reform suspends Scotland candidate less than a day after he was announced
headlines.description: Scotland leader Malcolm Offord told BBC Radio Scotland that the party had "spent a lot of time on vetting" candidates

headlines.title: Dame Jenni Murray, former BBC Woman's Hour presenter, dies at 75
headlines.description: She was the Radio 4 programme's longest-serving presenter, having left in 2020 after 33 years.

headlines.title: Fog and sunshine in the weekend forecast as high pressure clings on
headlines.description: After a week that brought the warmest weather of the year so far temperatures are set to drop a little but many places will remain dry as Ben Rich explains.

headlines.title: Socialists battle to hold Paris in key mayoral elections across France
headlines.description: Emmanuel Grégoire is facing a challenge from Rachida Dati on the right who is behind in the opinion polls.

headlines.title: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
headlines.description: Kyiv and Budapest disagree on how badly damaged a hub in western Ukraine bombed by Russia really is, as its oil flow remains suspended.

headlines.title: Luke Littler applies to trademark his face to combat AI fakes
headlines.description: The darts star's image has been used legally on darts merchandise, snacks and fashion brands.

headlines.title: Starmer most popular PM - in Toby Jug sales
headlines.description: Toby Jugs depicting Sir Keir outsold all other prime ministers in the Parliament shop in 2025.

headlines.title: Coroner 'not satisfied' boxing legend Ricky Hatton intended to take own life
headlines.description: Former boxing world champion Ricky Hatton was found "unresponsive" at his home in September.

headlines.title: I walked down aisle by myself on my wedding day after rare cancer diagnosis
headlines.description: Maariya Khan was left with minimal movement after needing surgery following her cancer diagnosis.

headlines.title: Zoo armadillos celebrate 19th birthdays
headlines.description: Dillon and Doris are said to be firm favourites with staff and visitors at Telford Exotic Zoo.

headlines.title: Secret tunnel to reopen after being hidden for 50 years
headlines.description: The grand Horsley Towers was once lived in by prominent 19th century mathematician Ada Lovelace.

headlines.title: Journalist becomes surprise kidney donor for teen
headlines.description: An Essex mum wrote to her local paper with a kidney donation appeal, and got more than she expected.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Week: The War In Iran Escalates
headlines.description: Has President Trump lost control of the war in Iran?

headlines.title: Trump threatens TV networks over unpatriotic Iran coverage
headlines.description: And why has top counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigned?

headlines.title: Man Utd fury at 'astonishing' and 'baffling' penalty decisions
headlines.description: Manchester United are left furious over "astonishing" refereeing decisions as two penalties were given  and one was not in their draw at Bournemouth.

headlines.title: Klopp, small shinpads and whether he really is boring - Milner in his own words
headlines.description: James Milner - the Premier League's record appearance holder, speaks to reporter Kelly Somers about his career - including a debut goal for hometown club Leeds, a dramatic Champions League semi-final comeback against Barcelona and returning from nine months out injured.

headlines.title: The indoor evolution threatening to outstrip outdoor golf
headlines.description: Whisper it quietly, but by the end of 2028 it is predicted there will be more rounds of virtual golf played in the UK than outdoors ones.

headlines.title: GB's Azu fourth at World Indoors in 'one of best 60m races of all time'
headlines.description: Defending champion Jeremiah Azu narrowly misses out on a medal in "one of the best 60m races of all time" at the World Athletics Indoor Championships.

headlines.title: Maguire sent off as Bournemouth fight back to draw with Man Utd
headlines.description: Despite taking the lead twice, Manchester United are held to a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth and have Harry Maguire sent off as they drop points in the race for a top-four finish in the Premier League.

headlines.title: Playing football and being a mum - Man Utd's Bizet's 'two dreams'
headlines.description: Manchester United and Norway winger Celin Bizet Donnum discusses how she's balancing her pregnancy along with a desire to get back on the football pitch.

headlines.title: Privilege, criticism & VAR - life as a boxing referee
headlines.description: Phil Edwards speaks to BBC Sport about what it's really like to be a boxing referee and whether he thinks VAR could work in the sport.
